text: The oxidative addition of Ni(I) to aryl iodides is widely employed in catalytic reactions; however, little is known about the mechanism. In this work, we investigated the oxidative addition process at a detailed level using a combination of electroanalytical and statistical methods. We were able to measure catalytic activity at a rate that is orders of magnitude faster than the existing methods, and we collected the largest data set of oxidative addition rates for a broad range of aryl iodides and four common ligand sets: Ni(<sup>Me</sup>BPy), Ni(<sup>Me</sup>Phen), Ni(Terpy), and Ni(BPP). With more than 200 rates collected using multiple sources and methods, we use multivariate linear regression to correlate the rates to the pertinent electronic and steric effects. The models led to a mechanism description of oxidative addition into three different paths that depend on the ligand set of the catalyst (halogen-atom abstraction or three-center concerted), and it allows us to construct a global heat map that was applicable to predict the reaction outcome in the Ni-based cross-coupling reactions, offering valuable insights on catalyst design.
